title: NIO, Intuit, And Reddit Are Among Top 10 Large-Cap Losers Last Week (May 18-May 22): Are The Others In Your Portfolio?
author: Nabaparna Bhattacharya
description: Large-cap stocks in software, electric vehicles, data centers, and healthcare faced significant pressure last week due to weak guidance, regulatory concerns, rising bond yields, and competitive risks. Technology and China-linked companies led the declines, with ten major stocks experiencing notable losses ranging from 4.96% to 18.28%.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Fell 18.28% after reporting Q3 results with Q4 and 2026 EPS guidance below estimates, plus 17% workforce reduction
Decreased 12.58% following Q1 results and regulatory pressure from China Securities Regulatory Commission on cross-border trading
Slid 10.87% following reports of Facebook launching a competing app
Decreased 13.9% last week
Slumped 11.57% as data center-related companies faced headwinds from rising bond yields affecting debt funding
Fell 12.46% last week amid healthcare sector pressure
Decreased 11.37% following first-quarter financial results reporting
Slumped 5.49% as China-linked stocks faced regulatory and competitive pressures
Slipped 7.98% as data center companies faced challenges from rising bond yields impacting funding for major projects
Slumped 4.96% despite TD Cowen raising price target, indicating broader market weakness

title: Tenet Healthcare Blames Admissions Mix For Soft Q1 Sales
author: Vandana Singh
description: Tenet Healthcare reported mixed Q1 2026 results with adjusted EPS of $4.82 beating consensus of $4.18, but sales of $5.37B missed expectations of $5.39B. The company blamed unfavorable payer mix from lower exchange admissions and winter storms for soft performance. Despite this, Tenet raised full-year adjusted earnings guidance to $16.38-$18.68 per share and reaffirmed sales guidance of $21.5-$22.3B. The stock declined 5.95% on the news.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mixed results with earnings beat offset by revenue miss and stock decline of 5.95%. While the company raised full-year earnings guidance and showed strong cash flow growth, management cited headwinds from unfavorable payer mix, winter storms, and cyber attacks. The technical indicators show bearish short-term momentum with the stock trading below key moving averages.

Mentioned briefly in a related article noting soft respiratory season and volume lags. Q1 2026 revenue grew 4.3% YoY to $19.11B with adjusted EPS of $7.15, but the company is flagging operational headwinds similar to Tenet, suggesting industry-wide challenges in the hospital sector.

title: Tenet Healthcare Quarterly Profit Surges As Hospital, Surgery Demand Grows
author: Vandana Singh
description: Tenet Healthcare reported strong Q4 2025 results with sales of $5.527 billion (up 8.9% YoY) and adjusted EPS of $4.70 (up from $3.44), beating consensus estimates. The company saw growth across both hospital operations and ambulatory segments, driven by higher acuity cases and favorable payer mix. For 2026, Tenet expects revenue guidance of $21.5-$22.3 billion and adjusted EBITDA of $4.485-$4.785 billion, with shares rising 6.73% on the news.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong Q4 earnings beat with 8.9% YoY revenue growth, significant EPS improvement (37% increase), robust EBITDA growth, and positive 2026 guidance. Growth across all segments with higher acuity cases and favorable payer mix indicate strong operational execution and market demand.

title: Why Tenet Healthcare Could Be the Sector’s Big Winner
author: Nabeel Bukhari
description: Tenet Healthcare demonstrates strong financial performance with impressive Q2 2025 results, featuring revenue growth, margin expansion, and strategic focus on ambulatory care. The company shows potential for growth despite potential future Medicaid policy challenges.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong Q2 performance with 3.2% revenue growth, 74% surge in adjusted EPS, expanding margins, robust cash flow, strategic share buybacks, and attractive valuation compared to peers
Consistent revenue growth but more expensive valuation compared to Tenet, with lower EBITDA margins and growth rates
Flat fundamentals, declining revenue and EBITDA, and weaker financial performance compared to Tenet

title: Tenet Revenue Tops Estimates in Q2
author: Jesterai
description: Tenet Healthcare reported strong Q2 2025 earnings, with adjusted EPS of $4.02, exceeding analyst expectations. The company saw revenue growth in Ambulatory Care and raised its full-year financial guidance, driven by margin improvements and strategic portfolio shifts.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Exceeded earnings expectations, raised full-year guidance, demonstrated revenue growth in Ambulatory Care segment (11.3%), improved margins, increased free cash flow by 23.4%, and strategic portfolio optimization
